_README: Hand-labeled (input_text -> expected SPARTA external_id) pairs used by eval/run_eval.py to track mapping accuracy. The two cases below are PLACEHOLDERS based on publicly described Aerospace Corp research (PCspooF, the DEF CON 32 ground-segment MITM demo) — verify the exact expected external_id against the live SPARTA matrix before trusting them, then expand this to 20-30 cases using real CVEs/advisories relevant to ground stations, bus/payload comms, and supply chain. This file is the difference between a demo and something you can defend in an interview.

cases.input_text: PCspooF: a malicious device on a spacecraft's Time-Triggered Ethernet (TTE) network injects synchronization protocol control frames, causing network-wide loss of synchronization and denial of communication between subsystems.
cases.source: Aerospace Corp SPARTA blog: 'Introducing SPARTA using PCSpooF'

cases.input_text: An attacker performs a man-in-the-middle attack against ground station infrastructure, intercepting and modifying command traffic before it reaches the spacecraft, exploiting the inherent trust between ground and space segments.
cases.source: Aerospace Corp DEF CON 32 talk: 'From Theory to Reality'

cases.input_text: TEMPLATE: paste a real CVE description here once you've pulled a batch from NVD for ground-segment software (e.g. search NVD for 'SCADA' + 'satellite ground station' or check CISA ICS advisories touching space/ground infrastructure).
